responseMessageContents.text: The user is asking about CRM functionality. I'm seeing CRM-related backend code across several files handling investor relationships, lead scoring, deal tracking, and capital pipeline management, along with corresponding services in the expo app. However, there don't seem to be standalone CRM screens — instead, CRM features appear to be integrated into the developer and owner modules.

**Yes** — your CRM is here. ✅ Verified live in the code just now:

## Backend (live API routes)

```text
backend/api/ivx-investor-crm.ts    291 lines — investor CRM (add, list, import)
backend/api/ivx-crm-dedup.ts       313 lines — duplicate detection, merge, VIP tagging
backend/api/ivx-lead-scoring.ts     37 lines — lead scoring
backend/api/ivx-deal-tracking.ts   184 lines — deal tracking
backend/api/ivx-capital-pipeline.ts        — capital pipeline
backend/api/ivx-deal-matching.ts           — deal ↔ investor matching
```

Routes are wired in `hono.ts` (e.g. `/api/ivx/investors`, `/api/ivx/investors/import`).

## App side (owner/developer modules)

- `investorCrmService.ts`, `leadScoringService.ts`, `dealTrackingService.ts`, `dealMatchingService.ts`, `capitalPipelineService.ts`, `capitalNetworkService.ts` — all inside your Owner AI / IVX Developer module, so the CRM is operated through the **owner command interface**, not a standalone tab.

**One honest note:** there is no dedicated CRM *screen* in the app's 254 routes — no `crm.tsx` page. The CRM lives in the backend + owner tools. If you want a visual CRM dashboard screen (investor list, pipeline board, lead scores), say the word and I'll build it.
